full_response:
We are given the 4-dimensional spacetime metric  
ds² = −(1 + r²/ℓ²) dt² + dr²/(1 + r²/ℓ²) + r² dΩ²  
and the statement that its Riemann tensor takes the form of a constant‐curvature (maximally symmetric) space,  
R_{abcd} = −(1/ℓ²) ( g_{ac} g_{bd} − g_{ad} g_{bc} ) .  

We wish to compute the Kretschmann scalar  
K ≡ R_{abcd} R^{abcd} .  

1. Write down R_{abcd} and its raised version R^{abcd}:  
   R_{abcd} = −ℓ^{−2}(g_{ac}g_{bd} − g_{ad}g_{bc})  
   ⇒ R^{abcd} = g^{a a′}g^{b b′}g^{c c′}g^{d d′}R_{a′b′c′d′}  
              = −ℓ^{−2}(g^{a c}g^{b d} − g^{a d}g^{b c})  

2. Form the contraction:  
   K = R_{abcd}R^{abcd}  
     = (−ℓ^{−2}) (−ℓ^{−2}) [ g_{ac}g_{bd} − g_{ad}g_{bc} ] [ g^{a c}g^{b d} − g^{a d}g^{b c} ]  
     = ℓ^{−4} [ ( g_{ac}g_{bd} )(g^{a c}g^{b d})  
                −2 ( g_{ac}g_{bd} )(g^{a d}g^{b c})  
                + ( g_{ad}g_{bc} )(g^{a d}g^{b c}) ]  

3. Evaluate each term in n = 4 spacetime dimensions:  
   (i)   g_{ac}g^{a c} = δ_c^c = 4  
         ⇒ (g_{ac}g_{bd})(g^{a c}g^{b d}) = (g_{ac}g^{a c})(g_{bd}g^{b d}) = 4·4 = 16  
   (ii)  g_{ac}g_{bd}g^{a d}g^{b c} = (g_{ac}g^{a d})(g_{bd}g^{b c})
         = δ_c^d δ_d^c = 4  
   (iii) (g_{ad}g_{bc})(g^{a d}g^{b c}) = (g_{ad}g^{a d})(g_{bc}g^{b c}) = 4·4 = 16  

   Putting these in gives:  
   K = ℓ^{−4} [ 16 − 2·4 + 16 ] = ℓ^{−4} (16 − 8 + 16) = 24 ℓ^{−4}  

Hence the Kretschmann scalar is  

\boxed{K = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} \;=\; \frac{24}{\ell^4}\,.}

Confidence: 100%
